implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0

completion_promises.criterion_text: _check_constitutional_override(target_identifier: str) -> GateDecision | None returns a fully-formed GateDecision for target_identifier in {"review_pr", "create_pr_after_review"} and None otherwise
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/constitutional.py defines CONSTITUTIONAL_OVERRIDE_TARGETS = frozenset({'review_pr', 'create_pr_after_review'}) and _check_constitutional_override which returns a fully-formed GateDecision when target_identifier is in the set and None otherwise. test_constitutional.py::TestCheckConstitutionalOverrideMatching parametrizes both matching identifiers and five non-matching identifiers (plus empty-string edge case) — all 8 cases pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Returned GateDecision has mode == GateMode.MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL, auto_approve_override is True, threshold_applied is None, and a rationale that names the constitutional rule
completion_promises.evidence: _check_constitutional_override constructs GateDecision with mode=GateMode.MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL, auto_approve_override=True, threshold_applied=None, and rationale=CONSTITUTIONAL_OVERRIDE_RATIONALE which includes 'ADR-ARCH-026'. test_constitutional.py::TestConstitutionalOverrideDecisionFields covers each field via parametrized tests for both targets — all pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: evaluate_gate() calls _check_constitutional_override as its first statement; if non-None, returns immediately without consulting coach_score, detection_findings, retrieved_priors, or calibration_adjustments
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/models.py evaluate_gate body now opens with a lazy import of _check_constitutional_override followed by the override call (passing only target_identifier, target_kind, stage_label, build_id, decided_at). If override is non-None, the function returns it before _validate_criterion_breakdown, prompt assembly, or the reasoning_model_call. test_constitutional.py::TestEvaluateGateConstitutionalFirst::test_evaluate_gate_first_statement_is_constitutional_override walks the function AST to assert override reference precedes any reasoning helper, and test_evaluate_gate_does_not_consult_reasoning_model_for_override passes a reasoning-model double that raises if invoked — passing means the model was never called.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group A scenario "A pull-request-review stage always requires human approval regardless of evidence" passes against coach_score=0.95, detection_findings=[], retrieved_priors=[concurring]
completion_promises.evidence: test_constitutional.py::TestEvaluateGateConstitutionalFirst::test_evaluate_gate_review_pr_returns_constitutional_decision invokes evaluate_gate with target_identifier='review_pr', coach_score=0.95, criterion_breakdown all 0.95, empty detection_findings, and a concurring PriorReference (relevance_score=0.95). Asserts mode=MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL, auto_approve_override=True, threshold_applied=None, coach_score=None (override short-circuits) — passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group C scenario "Creating a pull request after review is treated with the same constitutional rule" passes for target_identifier="create_pr_after_review"
completion_promises.evidence: test_constitutional.py::TestEvaluateGateConstitutionalFirst::test_evaluate_gate_create_pr_after_review_returns_constitutional_decision invokes evaluate_gate with target_identifier='create_pr_after_review' and coach_score=0.99 (would auto-approve absent override). Asserts mode=MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL, auto_approve_override=True, threshold_applied=None — passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group E @security @regression two-layer regression test: a pull-request-review stage produces MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL even when the prompt-layer SAFETY_CONSTITUTION is removed in the test harness (executor branch alone is sufficient)
completion_promises.evidence: test_constitutional.py::TestTwoLayerRegression::test_executor_branch_alone_produces_mandatory_human_approval is parametrized over both override targets. The harness simulates 'prompt layer disabled' by passing constitutional_rules=[] AND a reasoning_model_call double that raises if invoked — the executor branch is the only enforcement remaining. Both parametrized invocations pass with mode=MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL. A complementary AST-walk test (test_regression_signal_is_visible_when_executor_layer_disabled) pins the override-precedes-reasoning ordering so a future refactor surfaces the regression.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group C @negative scenario "A mandatory-human-approval decision must not masquerade as a threshold-based approval" passes — auto_approve_override=True AND threshold_applied is None invariant holds
completion_promises.evidence: test_constitutional.py::TestConstitutionalOverrideDecisionFields::test_negative_invariant_holds_for_both_targets iterates over CONSTITUTIONAL_OVERRIDE_TARGETS and asserts BOTH auto_approve_override is True AND threshold_applied is None hold simultaneously for every matching target. Pydantic's GateDecision._check_invariants validator (DM-gating §6 invariant 1) rejects MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL with auto_approve_override=False AND threshold_applied is not None, so any drift would fail at construction time as well.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests cover both target identifiers and at least three non-matching identifiers
completion_promises.evidence: test_constitutional.py::TestCheckConstitutionalOverrideMatching covers both target identifiers ('review_pr', 'create_pr_after_review') as parametrized matching cases AND five non-matching identifiers ('review_specification', 'review_design', 'review_build_plan', 'execute_subagent', 'search_graphiti') plus empty-string edge case — well above the three-non-matching threshold.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Module imports nothing from nats_core, nats-py, or langgraph
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/constitutional.py imports only from stdlib (datetime) and the sibling forge.gating.models. test_constitutional.py::TestModulePurity::test_constitutional_imports_are_clean parses constitutional.py with ast and asserts no import path begins with any of {'nats_core', 'nats', 'langgraph', 'forge.adapters'} — passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors

After applying `ruff format` to the test file (cosmetic only), all three files pass `ruff check` with 'All checks passed!' and `ruff format --check` reports no diffs. Full project test suite: 713 passed, 20 warnings (pre-existing pytest.mark.seam typos in unrelated files).
